perturbation.original_text: Unless expressly authorized in writing by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party agrees to retain the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not copy or disclose the Confidential Information to or use the Confidenti al Information for the benefit of any third party. Confidential Information shall only be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s employees and, even then, only to the extent that such employees have a specific need to know of the Confidential information, for the evaluation of the proposed transaction. Before receiving any part of the Confidential Information, Receiving Party’s employees shall be required to read this Non -disclosure Agreement and, by receiving such Confidential Information, such employee shall acknowledge and agree to abide by the Receiving Party’s obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless expressly authorized in writing by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party agrees to retain the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not copy or disclose the Confidential Information to or use the Confidenti al Information for the benefit of any third party. Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s employees. Before receiving any part of the Confidential Information, Receiving Party’s employees shall be required to read this Non -disclosure Agreement and, by receiving such Confidential Information, such employee shall acknowledge and agree to abide by the Receiving Party’s obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'only to the extent that such employees have a specific need to know of the Confidential information, for the evaluation of the proposed transaction' in Section 2, it weakens the restriction on disclosing Confidential Information to employees. The removal of this phrase contradicts with the initial purpose of a non disclosure agreement, in which this change makes the terms of the document very unclear and also makes the enforcement of the clause very hard. This now contradicts the NDA's overall purpose of protecting sensitive information, as there is no clear requirement regarding the purpose the employee need to know this information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless expressly authorized in writing by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party agrees to retain the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not copy or disclose the Confidential Information to or use the Confidenti al Information for the benefit of any third party. Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s employees. Before receiving any part of the Confidential Information, Receiving Party’s employees shall be required to read this Non -disclosure Agreement and, by receiving such Confidential Information, such employee shall acknowledge and agree to abide by the Receiving Party’s obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits the disclosure of confidential information to employees with a specific need to know for the evaluation of the transaction. The changed text removes this limitation, broadly permitting disclosure to employees. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text, because this change makes the requirements of the document unclear, and this goes directly against the purpose of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding any other provisions of the Agreement, each party acknowledges that Confidential Information shall not include any information which: a. was known to the Receivi ng Party prior to the disclosure hereunder; b. was received from a third party not under an obligation of confidence to Receiving Party; c. is in the public domain at the time of disclosure hereunder or subsequently entered in the public domain without the fault of the Receiving Party; d. has been independently developed by an employee of the Receiving Party that has not had access directly or indirectly to Confidential Information, and Receiving Party can substantiate any claim of independent development by writt en evidence; or e. is required to be disclosed by law, provided however that the Receiving Party shall give immediate notice of any such request for disclosure and cooperate with the Disclosing Party in its efforts to obtain a protective order or other protec tion from the requirement or consequences of disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding any other provisions of the Agreement, each party acknowledges that Confidential Information shall not include any information which: a. was known to the Receivi ng Party prior to the disclosure hereunder; b. was received from a third party not under an obligation of confidence to Receiving Party; c. is in the public domain at the time of disclosure hereunder or subsequently entered in the public domain without the fault of the Receiving Party; d. has been independently developed by an employee of the Receiving Party that has not had access directly or indirectly to Confidential Information; or e. is required to be disclosed by law, provided however that the Receiving Party shall give immediate notice of any such request for disclosure and cooperate with the Disclosing Party in its efforts to obtain a protective order or other protec tion from the requirement or consequences of disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'and Receiving Party can substantiate any claim of independent development by writt en evidence' from section 3d weakens the protection of confidential information. Without the requirement to substantiate independent development with written evidence, it becomes much easier for the Receiving Party to claim independent development, potentially circumventing the Non-Disclosure Agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction with the agreement's overall purpose of protecting confidential information, as it makes it significantly harder to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information is defined as any information, whether written or verbal, of either party hereto, (Disclosing Party) which is disclosed to o r observed by the other party (Receiving Party) in connection with or as a result of the evaluation of any possible transaction between the Univers ity of Arkansas at Little Rock and ______________________________ and which is, at the time of disclosure, marked as being Confident ial or Proprietary, or is reasonably identifiable as confidential, proprietary information of the Disclosing Party pertaining to information in the areas of (subject matter) : _______________________________________________ ___________________________. Such Confidential Information may include, but is not li mited to, business plans, forecasts, content, processes, projections or analysis, software, hardware, product, or system designs, specifications, documentation, code, structure, or protocols. Confidential Information that is disclosed verbally will also be included as proprietary.
perturbation.justification: The original text required written evidence to substantiate a claim of independent development, thus protecting the confidential information as defined in the contradicted text. The changed text removes this requirement, making it easier to claim independent development without proof. This weakens the protection of 'Confidential Information' as defined in the contradicted text, creating a direct conflict. The changed text introduces ambiguity that the contradicted text expects certainty and would cause confusion, unenforceability, or a legal issue within the document as Receiving party can easily claim confidential information as their own.

perturbation.original_text: Either party will be relieved of its obligation hereunder it, and to the extent, that Confidential Information is explicitly approved for release by written authorization of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party will be relieved of its obligation hereunder if Confidential Information is approved for release by authorization of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'explicitly' and 'written' this changes the agreement, in which now makes the clause more ambiguous. An 'authorization' can be written or verbal, creating an in-text contradiction with the original intent of the agreement to protect confidential information through clear, documented releases. There will not be a clear path to verify if the party was authorized.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless expressly authorized in writing by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party agrees to retain the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not copy or disclose the Confidential Information to or use the Confidenti al Information for the benefit of any third party. Confidential Information shall only be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s employees and, even then, only to the extent that such employees have a specific need to know of the Confidential information, for the evaluation of the proposed transaction. Before receiving any part of the Confidential Information, Receiving Party’s employees shall be required to read this Non -disclosure Agreement and, by receiving such Confidential Information, such employee shall acknowledge and agree to abide by the Receiving Party’s obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for 'explicitly' and 'written' authorization, which directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' requiring 'expressly authorized in writing'. The original text required 'explicitly approved for release by written authorization' while the changed text only requires 'Confidential Information is approved for release by authorization'. This creates a strong contradiction because the 'Contradicted Text' specifies that authorization must be 'in writing,' which is no longer guaranteed in the changed text. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' where essential language is removed, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall retain the t itle and full ownership rights to their respective “Confidential Information”.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall retain the title and ownership rights to their respective information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the word 'full' and “Confidential Information” weakens the ownership rights, potentially creating conflict over information shared. By removing the reference to 'Confidential Information,' the modified text creates ambiguity. Now it's unclear whether this applies to all information or just the defined Confidential Information. This omission contradicts the original purpose of the agreement, which is specifically about protecting confidential information. There is no description to what the word Information is refering too.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information is defined as any information, whether written or verbal, of either party hereto, (Disclosing Party) which is disclosed to o r observed by the other party (Receiving Party) in connection with or as a result of the evaluation of any possible transaction between the Univers ity of Arkansas at Little Rock and ______________________________ and which is, at the time of disclosure, marked as being Confident ial or Proprietary, or is reasonably identifiable as confidential, proprietary information of the Disclosing Party pertaining to information in the areas of (subject matter) : _______________________________________________ ___________________________. Such Confidential Information may include, but is not li mited to, business plans, forecasts, content, processes, projections or analysis, software, hardware, product, or system designs, specifications, documentation, code, structure, or protocols. Confidential Information that is disclosed verbally will also be included as proprietary.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specification of 'Confidential Information,' weakening ownership rights and creating ambiguity. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which provides a detailed definition of 'Confidential Information.' By broadening the scope to simply 'information,' the modified text conflicts with the original agreement's purpose of protecting specifically defined confidential information, and creates a contradiction by making the scope of protection unclear.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed, construed, and interpreted by the laws of the State of Arkansas without reference to its choice of laws principles.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted by the laws of the State of Arkansas.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'construed' and 'without reference to its choice of laws principles' removes specificity in how the agreement is interpreted. This creates an in-text contradiction as previously stated that the agreement will be construed based on the laws of Arkansas. Now that requirement is not there. By removing 'without reference to its choice of laws principles', the contract is now implying it could now use reference to its choice of law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted by the laws of the State of Arkansas.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the agreement would be construed based on Arkansas law, explicitly excluding reference to choice of law principles. The changed text removes 'construed' and 'without reference to its choice of laws principles.' This creates an in-text contradiction because the changed text removes the original limitation on how the agreement is interpreted, and now implies that choice of law principles might be considered, directly conflicting with the implied meaning of the original text. Although the changed and contradicted text are similar, the removal of specificity from the original language as part of the change and the explanation provided for why it is a contradiction implies it could now use reference to its choice of law, creating an in-text contradiction. Therefore, the omission causes a clear and direct conflict, triggering a contradiction based on the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.
